signals.title: USD/JPY — BoJ Hike + Intervention Risk + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.45 — BoJ raised rates to 1.0% (highest since 1995), narrowing the Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% carry differential to +275bp (from +300bp). Despite the hike, the pair has broken above 160.00, indicating carry trade resilience and potential for further BoJ intervention. JPY COT LF net short −115,036 (extreme crowded short) faces short-squeeze risk (USD/JPY downside if shorts cover) if BoJ signals further tightening or intervenes. Options market shows moderate put skew (25d RR -1.06) despite the price action, suggesting hedging for JPY strength. Watch for BoJ follow-up statements or direct intervention above 160.00.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Oil Plunge + Carry + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3993 — WTI Oil plunged -5.15% on reports of a US-Iran deal allowing Tehran to immediately sell oil, directly pressuring CAD. The USD/CAD vs WTI Oil correlation has broken significantly (corr=-0.286 vs 252d norm=-0.022, z=-1.60σ), indicating CAD is not benefiting from its typical inverse relationship with oil. The Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25% carry differential of +150bp continues to favor USD. CAD COT LF net short −60,671 (extreme short) suggests further downside risk if oil weakness persists. Watch for WTI Oil price stabilization or further supply news.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Pause + Negative CESI + Elevated Vol
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7074 — RBA paused rate hikes at 4.35% in June 2026 after three consecutive hikes, signaling a shift in monetary policy. This comes as AUD CESI is negative (net=-8 misses), indicating data is consistently disappointing expectations. Options market shows elevated vol premium (IV 21.3% vs HV30 8.1%, VRP +13.2%), suggesting strong hedging demand for AUD downside protection. The Fed 3.75% vs RBA 4.35% carry differential of -60bp still favors AUD, but the RBA pause and weak data could erode this advantage. Watch for RBA forward guidance at the next meeting on 11 Aug.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Retail Extreme + Negative CESI
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5838 — Retail sentiment shows an extreme long bias (85% long), which typically acts as a contrarian indicator for potential reversals. This aligns with a negative NZD CESI (net=-4 misses), indicating disappointing economic data. The RBNZ is on hold at 2.25% with a market bias for a hike, but the Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% carry differential of +150bp favors USD. If economic data continues to disappoint, retail longs face unwind risk. Watch for upcoming RBNZ statements for any shift in hawkish bias.
